hero.label: Services
hero.title: Car inspection, diagnostics and expert assessment
hero.subtitle: Used car inspection, OBD computer diagnostics, mileage and body check. So your decision is based on facts.

s1.title: Comprehensive pre-purchase car inspection
s1.text: Full on-site vehicle evaluation: body with paint thickness measurement, engine, transmission, suspension, electrical system, interior. Every element the seller might hope goes unnoticed is checked. The result: a documented report with photos and a clear recommendation.

s2.title: Computer diagnostics (OBD)
s2.text: Using professional OBD scanners, error codes are read and engine control, safety, comfort and transmission systems are checked. Suitable for diesel, petrol, hybrid and electric vehicles.

s3.title: Pre-deal consultation
s3.text: An opportunity to evaluate a specific listing from Autoplius, Autogidas or other platforms before visiting the car. Photos, price, and specifications are analyzed.

s4.title: Car buying and selling assistance
s4.text: Assistance in finding a reliable used car or selling a current one. The process is handled transparently, without intermediary schemes. We also advise on cars imported from Germany, the USA and other countries.

cta.title: Need a tailored solution?
cta.text: Get in touch. The service will be adapted to the specific situation.

checklist.label: Inspection scope
checklist.title: What is checked during every car inspection
checklist.item1: Body condition, paint thickness measurement, corrosion and accident signs
checklist.item4: Electrical system and OBD computer diagnostics
checklist.item8: Mileage analysis and vehicle history verification
checklist.item9: Test drive and road behaviour assessment
checklist.item10: Documented report with photos and clear recommendation

faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Frequently asked questions about car inspection
faq.q1: Is a pre-purchase car inspection really worth it?
faq.a1: You can skip it. Then you'll pay the mechanic instead, usually several times more. A car inspection isn't an expense. It's insurance against expensive surprises.
faq.q2: How long does the inspection take?
faq.a2: About 1.5–2 hours on average. It depends on the car and its condition. We don't rush — better to find a problem now than regret it later.
faq.q3: Do you inspect cars in any city in Lithuania?
faq.a3: Yes. We come to wherever the car is: at the seller's place, a car lot, or a service centre. No extra charge within Kaunas city and district. A travel fee applies outside the Kaunas area, agreed in advance.
faq.q4: What do I get after the car inspection?
faq.a4: A documented report with photos, identified defects, and a clear recommendation: buy, negotiate, or walk away.

hero.label: Process
hero.title: Clear structure, transparent result
hero.subtitle: From inquiry to report. Every stage is defined and understandable.

step1.title: Inquiry
step1.text: Contact is made by phone, email, or through the contact form. The vehicle and its location are specified.

step2.title: Scheduling
step2.text: Inspection time is arranged according to the client's availability. Inspections are conducted evenings and weekends as well.

step3.title: On-site arrival
step3.text: The inspection is performed at the vehicle's location: at the seller's, in a car lot, or at a service center.

step4.title: Inspection and diagnostics
step4.text: Systematic visual inspection, computer diagnostics, and test drive. Every element is checked according to an established methodology.

step5.title: Documented report
step5.text: A clear report is delivered with photos, identified defects, and an overall recommendation. No ambiguity.

step6.title: Client's decision
step6.text: All necessary information is provided. The decision to buy, negotiate, or walk away is made based on facts.

cta.title: The process starts with an inquiry
cta.text: One message or call is all it takes.
